questions.question: What primary limitation of standard HTTP requests do WebSockets overcome?
options: HTTP requests cannot transmit JSON data efficiently
HTTP is strictly unidirectional (client requests, server responds); the server cannot proactively push data to the client without a request
HTTP demands all data be securely encrypted using TLS
HTTP is restricted to a maximum of 4 request headers
questions.answer: HTTP is strictly unidirectional (client requests, server responds); the server cannot proactively push data to the client without a request
questions.explanation: In standard HTTP, the server cannot say 'Hey client, here makes a new chat message!' unless the client specifically asks. WebSockets maintain an open, bi-directional pipe allowing the server to push new data instantly.

questions.question: Why is Socket.io generally preferred over the native browser WebSocket API in production?
options: It uses a completely different protocol that is 100x faster than WebSockets
It provides essential resilience features like automatic reconnections and graceful fallback to HTTP long-polling if a corporate firewall blocks standard WebSocket ports
Native WebSockets only work in the Chrome browser
Socket.io automatically translates MySQL queries into JSON
questions.answer: It provides essential resilience features like automatic reconnections and graceful fallback to HTTP long-polling if a corporate firewall blocks standard WebSocket ports
questions.explanation: While native WebSockets are great, they involve heavy manual boilerplate to handle broken connections. Socket.io handles dropped WiFi reconnections automatically and ensures older/restrictive networks can still communicate.

questions.question: When setting up Socket.io with Express, why must you attach Socket.io to a manually created `httpServer = createServer(app)` instead of directly to the `app`?
options: Because Express is an asynchronous framework and Socket.io is synchronous
Because Socket.io requires the underlying core HTTP Node server instance to intercept the 'Upgrade' header that transitions an HTTP connection into a WebSocket connection
Because Express cannot route traffic to ports other than 80
Because creating a new server bypasses CORS restrictions
questions.answer: Because Socket.io requires the underlying core HTTP Node server instance to intercept the 'Upgrade' header that transitions an HTTP connection into a WebSocket connection
questions.explanation: A WebSocket starts its life as a standard HTTP GET request with an 'Upgrade: websocket' header. Socket.io needs access to the raw HTTP server, not just the Express routing layer, to process this protocol upgrade.

questions.question: What is the difference between `io.emit()` and `socket.broadcast.emit()`?
options: `io.emit()` sends only text; `socket.broadcast.emit()` sends binary buffers
`io.emit()` sends the message to every connected client; `socket.broadcast.emit()` sends the message to every connected client EXCEPT the sender
`io.emit()` sends to a specific room; `socket.broadcast` sends to all rooms
They are entirely identical in behavior
questions.answer: `io.emit()` sends the message to every connected client; `socket.broadcast.emit()` sends the message to every connected client EXCEPT the sender
questions.explanation: If User A sends a chat message, you might use socket.broadcast so Users B and C receive it, but User A doesn't get an echo of their own message back from the server.

questions.question: If you deploy your Node.js application to three separate servers (horizontal scaling) behind a Load Balancer, what critical problem occurs with Socket.io, and how is it solved?
options: The Load Balancer crashes; Solved by using UDP instead of TCP
Users on Server 1 cannot receive WebSocket messages broadcasted by Users on Server 2; Solved by connecting all servers using the Socket.io Redis Adapter
The JWT tokens become invalid; Solved by sharing the secret key
The database becomes locked; Solved by using SQLite
questions.answer: Users on Server 1 cannot receive WebSocket messages broadcasted by Users on Server 2; Solved by connecting all servers using the Socket.io Redis Adapter
questions.explanation: WebSockets are stateful tcp connections locked into the RAM of the specific server they connected to. A Redis database acts as a Pub/Sub middleman, synchronizing WebSocket 'emits' across all your physical servers.
